title: Roger Taplin
content:
Partner | Co-leader, Global Metals & Mining
People.ContactEmail rtaplin@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2003
- University of Witwatersrand
Creative solutions, industry knowledge and a business-focused approach.
Named one of the Top 25 Most Influential Lawyers in Canada (2025), Roger* is a sought after international mining transactional lawyer and is the Co-leader of the Global Metals & Mining Group. Roger's clients benefit from his broad range of experience and deep industry knowledge to manage their complex mining transactions internationally.
Clients trust Roger's strong negotiating skills and holistic approach to successfully guide them through public and private mining M&A transactions, including takeovers, business combinations, spin-outs and strategic investments. Known for his specialist knowledge of joint ventures and metal streaming, Roger regularly acts for mining companies and financiers in relation to sizeable mining projects globally.

#### Selected Recent Transactions
- Foran Mining – C$3.8 billion acquisition by Eldorado Gold.
- Royal Gold – Acquisition of Sandstorm Gold for US$3.5B and Horizon Copper for US$196M; C$200 million acquisition of Great Bear Royalties.
- CMOC – US$1.015 billion acquisition of Equinox Gold’s Brazil operations; C$581 million acquisition of Lumina Gold;  US$475 million sale of CMOC’s interest in Northparkes mine (Australia) to Evolution Mining.
- Gold Fields – C$2.16 billion acquisition of Osisko Mining.
- Glencore – US$7.3 billion acquisition of Teck’s steelmaking coal business, Elk Valley Resources (Canada); US$73 million acquisition of PolyMet Mining; C$53 million acquisition of Noranda Income Fund; approximately US$630 million acquisitions resulting in 100% ownership of the MARA copper project (Argentina).
- Silvercorp Metals – C$200 million acquisition of Adventus Mining; proposed C$120 million share‑exchange acquisition of Guyana Goldfields.
- Gatos Silver – US$970 million acquisition by First Majestic.
- SSR Mining – C$2.41 billion merger of equals with Alacer Gold.
- Wheaton Precious Metals – US$4.3 billion silver stream with BHP on Antamina (Peru); approximately US$3.3 billion in gold or silver streams in Spring Valley (USA), Kurmuk (Ethiopia), Koné (Côte d’Ivoire), Cangrejos (Ecuador), Marathon (Canada), Goose (Canada), Fenix Gold (Chile), Curipamba (Ecuador) and Blackwater (Canada); US$530 million purchase of metal streams from Orion (South Africa, Canada, Northern Ireland); US$135 million termination of Keno Hill silver stream (Canada).
- Royal Gold – US$1 billion gold stream on Kansanshi (Zambia); US$200 million gold stream and royalty on Warintza (Ecuador); US$265 million silver stream on Khoemacau (Botswana).
- Glencore – US$900 million silver stream on Antamina with Wheaton Precious Metals (Peru); US$500 million gold and silver stream on Antapaccay with Franco‑Nevada (Peru).
- Vale – US$690 million cobalt stream with Wheaton and Cobalt 27 on Voisey’s Bay (Canada).
- Sibanye‑Stillwater – US$500 million precious metals stream with Franco‑Nevada covering Marikana, Kroondal and Rustenburg operations (South Africa).
- CMOC – US$550 million gold and silver stream with Triple Flag on Northparkes (Australia).
- Ecora – US$50 million copper stream with Moxico Resources in Mimbula mine (Zambia).
- Newmont – US$804 million acquisition of a 70% interest in Red Chris (Canada); US$3.5 billion Neuvo Union joint venture with Teck (Chile); formation of Norte Abierto joint venture (Chile) with Barrick through acquisitions of assets from Barrick and Kinross.
- Gold Fields –C$700 million 50/50 Windfall project partnership (Canada).
- Paulson & Co . – US$1.1 billion joint acquisition with NovaGold of Barrick’s 50% interest in Donlin Gold (USA).
- Vale – US$3.4 billion strategic partnership with Manara Minerals involving Vale Base Metals (Global).
- Glencore – US$2 billion strategic alliance with Vale Base Metals to develop copper deposits in Sudbury (Canada); US$300 million BaseCore royalty and streaming joint venture with Ontario Teachers’ (Global); US$418 million spin‑out of Rosh Pinah (Namibia) and Perkoa (Burkina Faso).
- Anglo American – U$1.425 billion Pebble joint venture with Northern Dynasty (USA); US$57.3 million earn‑in on the Pegasus project (Ecuador); formation and ultimate disposal of Peace River Coal Partnership (Canada); multiple other exploration joint ventures (Global).
- Foran Mining – C$350 million non‑brokered private placement.
- B2Gold – US$90 million sale of a royalty portfolio to Sandbox Royalties; US$60 million acquisition of a 50% interest in Garamalote (Colombia).
- Rio Tinto – Updated US$2.3 billion funding plan for Oyu Tolgoi mine (Mongolia).
- Glencore – Backstop of C$7.6 billion rights offering by Katanga Mining and US$265 million rights offering by PolyMet Mining.
- Mubadala – US$130 million strategic investment in Equinox Gold (Global).
- Stelco – US$100 million option to acquire a 25% interest in the Minntac iron ore mine (USA).
*denotes Law Corporation
